Management to do the following courses of action:
1.	The Municipal Accountant must immediately prepare the liquidation reports of the total positive variance concerning the foregoing projects and submit them to COA for receipt (stamped) and signature by the auditor in the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI), if applicable, and Statement of Expenditures (SOE) before forwarding it to the Provincial Accountant.
Further, with fund transfers, reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts between the source agency and LGU-Cabanglasan must be done regularly in order to assure uniformity of the balances and to insure correctness, reliability and accuracy of the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant must assure that the reciprocal accounts balances are similar at the end of each period. If necessary, the Municipal Accountant should often request a copy of subsidiary ledger from the source agency for reference.
b. Management must immediately submit the hard copies of the various Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) of the previously cited projects to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification and for reference in conducting more audit procedures.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer-OIC to issue a check immediately from TF-NHA IP to TF-Regular bank account amounting to ₱ 5,804,749.58 to replenish the erroneous charging and utilization of funds.
We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor to expedite the full implementation of the previous NHA project with an outstanding Cash in Bank balance of ₱ 2,052,961.75. Otherwise, seek permission and approval from the National Housing Authority (NHA) higher authorities should the LGU wishes to utilize the same for another relevant program, project or activity.
Further, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer-OIC must reconcile their records and cashbooks regularly in order to establish correct, accurate and reliable Cash in Bank balance both in the Trust Fund books of account and in the cashbooks of the MTO.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the said financial grant immediately as receivable under the account “Due from NGOs/POs” and to treat similar transactions in the future as receivable rather than outright expense.
Further, management should require the Philippine National Red Cross (PNRC) to submit liquidation report of the ₱ 500,000.00 after the construction is complete soon to assure that the fund is used properly and according to its original purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to transfer the financial assistance of ₱ 1,438,980.00 to the Trust Fund immediately to protect the fund from possible misuse and misappropriation and to assure that its intended beneficiaries receive it in due time.
Also, the Municipal Accountant should maintain a separate subsidiary ledger in the “Due to NGAs” account in the Trust Fund pertaining to the said assistance to keep track its utilization and charges.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the entries in the “Due to BIR” account immediately and establish the breakdown or the alpha-list comprising the ₱ 2,521,123.22.  Also, the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers of the taxes withheld for easy monitoring and verification.
In addition, the Municipal Accountant should remit the total outstanding tax balance of ₱ 2,521,123.22 to Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) once the alpha-list is properly generated and the account is reconciled. Taxes withheld should always be remitted regularly and fully as mandated by the foregoing provision.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to do the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant must immediately prepare the liquidation reports of the total positive variance concerning the foregoing projects and submit them to COA for receipt (stamped) and signature by the auditor in the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI), if applicable, and Statement of Expenditures (SOE) before forwarding it to the Provincial Accountant.
Further, with fund transfers, reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts between the source agency and LGU-Cabanglasan must be done regularly in order to assure uniformity of the balances and to insure correctness, reliability and accuracy of the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant must assure that the reciprocal accounts balances are similar at the end of each period. If necessary, the Municipal Accountant should often request a copy of subsidiary ledger from the source agency for reference.
Management must immediately submit the hard copies of the various Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) of the previously cited projects to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification and for reference in conducting more audit procedures.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Officer (MEEDO) should prepare a ledger for each project under their management and supervision and update them regularly.
The MEEDO should strengthen the office internal control and closely monitor all cost/charges of each project based on the duly approved Program of Works. They should be inputted in their corresponding ledger religiously.
The MEEDO should refrain from charging unrelated expenses to another Trust Fund project so as not to disrupt its implementation, not to hamper its timely completion, not to defeat its true purpose and not to commit technical malversation.
The Municipal Accountant should carefully classify the nature of the disbursement and examine the documentary supports such as paid labor payrolls, etc. of every disbursement to ascertain that they are properly recorded in their appropriate accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to revisit IPSAS 24 or the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA), to be consistent with the presentation of actual amounts and to insure that they are directly related to the final budgeted amounts.
Further, they should strictly observe Section 13 of IPSAS 24 to ascertain the fair presentation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and to prevent misleading information that might detrimentally affect the decisions of the end-users.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the BAC Chairman to submit a written valid justification of the sudden alteration with the original contract time in the contract agreement and the necessary documents for the extension of contract time to the Office of the Auditor for further review and validation. If this is not possible, management should demand for the payment of the liquidated damages from the winning contractor.
Also, we recommended to the BAC Chairman to be consistent with the terms and conditions such as contract time during the whole duration of the bidding process and refrain from altering directly the contract time that was originally stipulated in the contract of agreement without undertaking the proper procedure on extension of contract time to be facilitated by the Municipal Engineer and as mandated in Section 10.1, Annex E of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 in order to prevent suspension or disallowance, if warranted, in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman the following courses of action:
To submit the required Supplemental/Bid Bulletin immediately to the Office of the Auditor to complete the auditorial and legal review of the contract and so that the COA Region 10-Technical Services Office (TSO) can proceed with its technical review.
To be timely with the submission of the copy of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. Ideally, it should be within 5 days after the execution of contract as provided in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
To observe strictly Section 22.5 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations in case there will be amendments with the Invitation to Bid and bidding documents in the future during the bidding process.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Vice Mayor the following courses of action:
To spearhead the enactment of SB Resolutions pertaining to the utilization of the 109,848 square meters land owned by the LGU at Barangay Poblacion, Cabanglasan, Bukidnon as housing site and the designation of a permanent Project Implementation Team for the project.
To secure a copy of every Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered into by LGU-Cabanglasan and carefully review its provisions for possible requirement of Sangguniang Resolutions and immediately pass them on as agreed to ascertain the propriety, validity and legality of the engagement or venture.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Acting MDRRMO to refrain from charging to 70 % LDRRMF any expense that is not directly related to disaster risk management of the LGU and strictly observe Section 4.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 as to its utilization to prevent suspension and disallowance in audit.
Further, if the LGU has any augmentation or re-alignment of programs, projects or activities in the future, management must do it in the proper and valid way or thru an approved supplemental budget. Management must refrain from charging random expenses directly to available current appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall direct the officials and employees concerned to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption to the auditor for review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare and to review the Bank Reconciliation Statements carefully particularly in dealing and treating various reconciling items with enormous amounts like the aforementioned in order to ascertain the propriety and reliability of the reports and to avoid unnecessary blunder that might cause confusion and misinterpretation to the end-users.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to connect and coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office to reconcile the material discrepancy of ₱ 9,026,007.78 and insure that a correct, updated balance of the said reciprocal accounts is established in order to ascertain an accurate “Due to LGUs” account in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to recognize the completed projects to their appropriate asset accounts in the Trust Fund books duly supported with the proper documentary requirements and submit the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and their pertinent documents to COA for review and verification. Along with the aforementioned documents, working paper detailing the complete data and information pertinent to the adjustments should likewise be submitted.

We also recommended to the Municipal Accountant to avoid directly closing the Construction in Progress accounts to the Due to NGA and Due to LGU accounts in the Trust Fund books. The Construction in Progress and Due to NGA/LGU accounts should instead be closed to their appropriate Asset and Government Equity accounts respectively in accordance to PPSAS suggested entries.

We recommended to the Municipal Engineer to submit all the required documents of the completed projects such as 100% Statements of Work Accomplished (SWA), Certificate of Project Completion and Contractor’s Bill to the Municipal Accountant as supports to the expected transfer of CIP to their appropriate asset accounts.
The Municipal Accountant must also prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) with complete supporting documents to adjust the CIP and submit the same to COA for further evaluation. Along with the aforementioned documents, working paper detailing the complete data and information pertinent to the adjustments should likewise be submitted for reference during verification and validation of data.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry voucher immediately to record in the Trust Fund books of accounts the Accounts Payable of the LGU towards the winning supplier and to recognize CIP – Buildings and Other Structures amounting to, roughly, ₱ 2.804 million duly supported with all the required documentary requirements such as original copies of delivery receipts, unpaid disbursement voucher, inspection and acceptance report, Purchase Request, Purchase Order, bidding documents and the like.

The Municipal Accountant must reconcile her records with that of the Municipal Engineering Office to settle the variance of ₱ 512,629.88 including the excess of ₱ 23,483.00 in the appropriation with the Municipal Budget Officer this time.
Moreover, the Municipal Engineer should always furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with its quarterly accomplishment report of the LGU’s projects for the purpose of check and balance and for reconciliation of records.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documentary supports of all the transactions listed in the schedule immediately to enable the auditor to continue with the validation of the year-end payables as to their existence, correctness and veracity, otherwise, make an adjusting entry and revert the same to surplus.

We further recommended to the Municipal Accountant to insure that for the succeeding years Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) of accounts payable are duly supported with the required documentary requirements abovementioned. As an internal auditor of the agency, the required payable documents must be under her custody for safekeeping and must be readily available for review and verification upon request by the auditor to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to start the recognition and recording of the 10% retention money in every Trust Fund infrastructure project separately and specifically in Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable in order to properly account subsequent similar transactions.
We further recommended to the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the account “Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable” for every infrastructure project for easy reference and monitoring.

We recommended to the management to submit a legal basis or a written justification of the said disbursement. Moreover, we recommended that the LGU limits the use of the SEF to what is only prescribed by law and to strictly adhere to its pertinent guidelines on its proper utilization to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

We recommended to the Municipal Engineer to insure that the specific provisions stipulated in the approved Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) of every Trust Fund program, project or activity to be implemented by LGU Cabanglasan are strictly adhered and attained. Also, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant being the Internal Auditor of the LGU, to refer to the documents such as MOA and Work and Financial Plan (WFP) before facilitating payments under the Trust fund.

Moreover, the management must also submit a revised MOA duly approved by the Provincial Governor inclusive of the school repair at Miaray, Cabanglasan to make the said project valid.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:

In lieu of the General Services Officer, the Municipal Mayor must assigned a focal person who will oversee and monitor the acceptance, inspection, safekeeping and issuance of the said construction materials inventories and who will devise efficient and effective methods and develop related internal control measures such as maintenance of inventory ledger cards, preparation of Requisition Issue Slips (RIS), etc. to guarantee that everything will be fully accounted and taken care of.
The Municipal Mayor must provide a secure and spacious warehouse that will accommodate and house the existing unutilized construction materials of the LGU.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Account must compel the barangays concerned to fully liquidate all of their outstanding fund transfer balances in compliance to the duly acknowledged and signed Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) by submitting liquidation reports complete with the apt documentary supports. She should immediately follow-up with another set of demand letters their inaction and passiveness.
The Municipal Mayor should suspend the grant of financial assistance to barangays with previously unliquidated fund transfers.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the total unrecorded land value of ₱ 3,696,281.70 in the books of accounts of LGU Cabanglasan in order to properly reflect the correct balance of the Land account in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
To present the Certificates of Title (COTs) of the ₱ 349,947.00 worth of land (Appendix I) as concrete confirmations for control and ownership. For those which have none, process and secure immediately their COTs in favor of the Municipality of Cabanglasan.
The Municipal Accountant must reconcile regularly with the Municipal Assessor’s register of lands to assure that everything that the LGU owns will all be accounted for and to guarantee that they are duly supported with the necessary documents in establishing their control and ownership.
The safekeeping of the Certificates of Title must be assumed by the Municipal Treasurer in the absence of the General Services Officer and must be filed in an orderly manner for easy review and verification when the need to do so arises in the near future. They must be kept in a staunch safe to save them from mutilation and unwarranted deterioration like the most common cases of termites’ infestation, fire infiltration and unforeseen soiling.

We recommended to the management the following courses of action:
The Municipal Mayor must organize a Local Road Network Inventory Committee to spearhead and oversee the conduct of physical count of all the municipal roads owned and maintained by LGU Cabanglasan and immediately prepare and submit to COA a Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN)or Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008. The Inventory Committee must coordinate with the MPDO for the list of the municipal roads and update it for any additions.
The Municipal Mayor must prioritize the creation of a General Services Office (GSO) and assigned a qualified and eligible individual for the position of General Services Officer who will facilitate with the preparation and submission of Report on Local Road Network (RLRN) or Annex A and the maintenance of the Local Road Network Property Card or Annex E of COA Circular 2015-008. Other responsibilities of the General Services Officer are enumerated in the said Circular.
The Municipal Accountant must faithfully observed Annex B in disclosing the details to the Notes to Financial Statements and she must maintain and update regularly Annex C or the prescribed Local Road Network Ledger Card as mentioned in the same Circular.
The LGU must closely observe the other significant provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 and execute them accordingly in order to properly account and report all the road networks owned and maintained by the LGU.

The Municipal Accountant must submit liquidation reports of the foregoing financial transactions duly supported but not limited to the following documents: utilization and consumption schedules, distribution lists, acceptance reports and 100 % Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA).

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the required subsidiary ledgers of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management to:
Require the concerned Accountable Officers to liquidate immediately their cash advances and demand refunds of the unused balances, otherwise, cause the withholding of their salaries and wages to compel them to settle their account.
Require the concerned Barangays, and Associations to liquidate immediately their fund transfers and demand refunds of the unused balances. No additional fund transfers shall be allowed unless previous fund transfers have been fully liquidated.
Exert extra efforts towards collections through cash settlements of long overdue unliquidated cash advances of officials and employees who are no longer connected with the municipality;
Request for write-off of long outstanding cash advances whose creditors are no longer located/deceased, if warranted; and
Enforce strict compliance to the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Require the Supply Officer/Designated GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards to be reconciled with the records of the Accounting Office. The Inventory Committee shall see to it that physical count of property, plant and equipment be conducted as well as and submission the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of the following year be assured.
Require the concerned Accounting staff and the GSO staff to reconcile regularly their records to determine the actual balances of PPE in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts at year-end. The balances in the RPCPPE and the accounting records should be investigated and adjusted accordingly;

We require the management to secure adequate property bond of its Property Officer commensurate to the property accountabilities and insure all insurable physical assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992, to protect the University’s assets in case of damage/loss due to fortuitous events.

We require that the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Financial statements will be submitted on time or on before February 14 of the ensuing year, as required in Section 41 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 347 of R.A. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2010-01.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Other concerned responsible officers to strictly adhere with the requirements on the submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Paragraph No. 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 122 of PD 1445 and other existing rules and regulations.

Require the Local School Board to:
Submit AIP and APP of the above expenses, pursuant to Article 410 of the IRR of R.A. No. 7160;
Submit justification for the inclusion of the above items totaling P103,646.00 in the CY 2017 SEF appropriation.
Comply with the provisions of DepEd, DBM, and DILG issued Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and allocate expenses only for the items identified as chargeable to the SEF.
Avoid inclusion of items in generic term in the appropriation.

We require the Municipality through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to:
Include among its member the Chairperson of the Sangguniang Kabataan Federation;
Review and update the Municipal Solid Waste Management ten (10) Year Plan in accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) annotated outline as required under Section 16 of RA 9003; and

Improved the current Material Recovery Facility by sorting out the materials and building of the required cells.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
the LGU should strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 114 and 121 of the NGAS Manual.
the accountant
should stop the practice of recording/charging regular purchases of supplies and materials immediately to the expense accounts;
should maintain on a current basis in the General Ledger (GL) and Inventory Account for all purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period. Issuance thereof shall be recorded based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued;
should maintain and update regularly the Supplies Ledger Card for each inventory stock which shall contain the details of the GL accounts and to reconcile their cards/records regularly with that of the Supply Section.
the LGU must also designate General Services Officer to:
conduct and inventory of supplies and materials annually and prepare a Report on the Physical Count of Inventories by type of inventory as of given date;
prepare on a monthly basis a RSMI based in the Requisition and Issue Slips; and
maintain Stock Cards for inventories, to be reconciled with the records of the Accounting Office regularly.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Local Government of Cabanglasan shall:
See to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and
Implement immediately the programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance of P12,444,873.25 as embodied in the Annual Investment plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall maintain/prepare another Cashbook - Cash In Treasury for collections and deposits under fund “Economic Enterprise” immediately after the cash examination or effective August 1, 2015.
We recommended that the liquidating officer shall perform the procedures in checking and verifying the RCD of the collectors and sign the certification portion of all the copies of the RCD

We recommended that all collectors must prepare and maintain a cashbook for each fund showing debit column for collections, credit column for remittances and a running balance column representing the difference between the debit/credit columns.

We recommended that management must conduct pre-repair and post-repair inspection of all repaired government vehicles to ensure that the said repair was necessary, done in accordance to the specification of the job order/contract and is 100% done. The results of the pre-repair and post-repair inspections must be written officially in the prescribed form.

We recommended that the project engineer of the LGU shall check the contractor’s SWA and certify the amount to be paid to the contractor as progress payment. 
	Likewise, we recommended that the documentary requirements as mentioned in Section 9.1.1 of COA Cir 2012-01 on progress billing must be complied and computation of the same must be checked and reviewed by the Municipal Accounting Office. 
Further, we recommended that the same will be followed for all the coming progress billings of all its infrastructure projects under straight contract.
